SUPPLEMENTAL APPENDIX OF FEDERAL AUTHORITIES CITED IN SUPPORT OF CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ON 4/28/08 DR. LAKOWSKY ISSUED A NOTE STATING THAT PLAINTIFF NEEDED A WORK CHAIR WITH A BACKREST. THE DOCTORS' NOTES ISSUED 5/08 AND 8/08 WERE ADDRESSING HIS SHOULDER INJURY. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS DISABLED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT REASONABLY ACCOMMODATED PLAINTIFF AND WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS IN GOOD FAITH GIVEN DEFENDANT'S DELAY OF APPROXIMATELY ONE YEAR TO RESPOND MEANINGFULLY TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT REGARDING THE LACK OF ERGONOMIC TRAINING/CHAIR WAS DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE CUSHION ISSUE AND WOULD HAVE BEEN UNCOVERED IN THE COURSE OF THE DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION INVESTIGATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) AS TO DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, COURT SUSTAINED ONLY AS TO OBJECTION #8 & #10. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Aaron S. Gorfein, Esq. of Law Office of Elizabeth F. McDonald 512-7788 for pltf & opposing party; Alexander Hernaez, Esq. of Fox Rothchild LLP 364-5540 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ON 4/28/08 DR. LAKOWSKY ISSUED A NOTE STATING THAT PLAINTIFF NEEDED A WORK CHAIR WITH A BACKREST. THE DOCTORS' NOTES ISSUED 5/08 AND 8/08 WERE ADDRESSING HIS SHOULDER INJURY. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS DISABLED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT REASONABLY ACCOMMODATED PLAINTIFF AND WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS IN GOOD FAITH GIVEN DEFENDANT'S DELAY OF APPROXIMATELY ONE YEAR TO RESPOND MEANINGFULLY TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT REGARDING THE LACK OF ERGONOMIC TRAINING/CHAIR WAS DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE CUSHION ISSUE AND WOULD HAVE BEEN UNCOVERED IN THE COURSE OF THE DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION INVESTIGATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) As to defendant's objections to evidence, Court sustained only as to objection #8 & #10. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
